evals.name: mixed-property-batch
evals.prompt:
It's the May 2026 close and I need to book this month's property bills as journal entries — give me a table I can review before posting. All amounts are AED and include 5% VAT where applicable:
- DEWA electricity & water for unit A-1203 (rev-share, owner Khan): AED 1,260
- Turnover cleaning by SparkleClean for unit A-1203: AED 420
- Plumbing repair by FixIt at unit A-1203 (rev-share): AED 735
- DEWA for unit L-0907 (this is one of our leased units): AED 980
- Emicool district cooling for unit B-0801 (rev-share, owner Mensah): AED 588
evals.expected_output: Five balanced journal entries using Silkhaus GL codes. A-1203 DEWA -> Landlord Receivable Utilities (120060) recoverable; SparkleClean cleaning -> Unit Cleaning Expenses (620013) Silkhaus-borne; FixIt plumbing -> Landlord Receivable R&M (120059) recoverable; L-0907 DEWA -> DEWA Consumption (640004) Silkhaus-borne (lease); Emicool -> Landlord Receivable Utilities (120060) recoverable. Each splits 5% Input VAT (110038) and credits Accounts Payable (110001). Every entry balances; a totals block foots.

evals.name: email-to-master
evals.prompt:
I pulled two vendor invoices out of my inbox — can you build an invoice master row for each and then the journal entries? Here they are:

--- Email 1 ---
From: noreply@dewa.gov.ae
Subject: Your DEWA Tax Invoice is ready
Tax Invoice No: 700114882
Billing Period: 01 May 2026 - 31 May 2026
Premise No: 2049913 (Marina Gate Tower 1, Apt 1203)
Consumption charges: AED 900.00
Municipality housing fee: AED 45.00
VAT (5%): AED 47.25
Total due: AED 992.25

--- Email 2 ---
From: accounts@sparkleclean.ae
Subject: Invoice SC-2207 - Cleaning services
Invoice SC-2207, dated 25/05/2026
Deep clean + turnover, Apt 1203 Marina Gate
Amount: AED 400.00
VAT 5%: AED 20.00
Total: AED 420.00

For context: Apt 1203 Marina Gate (premise 2049913) is our rev-share unit A-1203, owner Khan.
evals.expected_output: An invoice-master table with two rows (DEWA + SparkleClean) populated with vendor, category, invoice_no, dates, premise_ref, unit_id A-1203, unit_type revshare, amounts, vat_treatment standard. Then journal entries: DEWA recoverable -> Landlord Receivable Utilities (120060) + Input VAT (110038) / A/P (110001); cleaning -> Unit Cleaning Expenses (620013) Silkhaus-borne + Input VAT / A/P. Both balance. Note DEWA has consumption + municipality components.

evals.name: ambiguity-flags
evals.prompt: Quick one — book this maintenance invoice: a smart-lock company 'LockSmart Ltd' (they're based in the UK) billed us AED 1,000 with no VAT shown, for a lock replacement at unit C-0500. I'm honestly not sure right now whether C-0500 is a leased unit or a rev-share one.
evals.expected_output: A balanced entry that (a) applies reverse-charge VAT because the vendor is international/UK (Dr Input VAT 110038 / Cr Output VAT 110040 at 5%), (b) handles the unknown unit type by posting a sensible default and RAISING a review flag rather than silently guessing, and (c) flags that recoverability depends on lease-vs-rev-share. Should not invent a GL code; should surface the assumptions for the controller.
